top_signals.title: Germany’s Weirdest Debt Market May No Longer Be Safe Haven for Lenders
top_signals.summary: After storied Austrian motorcycle manufacturer KTM AG presented an insolvency plan to creditors last year, several of them convened a video conference to discuss a counter-proposal. More than 100 parties joined, but none of the usual big corporate lenders were involved. Instead, the group featured a motley crew of backers from tiny German towns, Chinese banks and European pension funds, all with wildly varying knowledge of the case at hand. The call was raucous, according to people briefed on th
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: UK investors need to stop buying rubbish stocks
top_signals.summary: Retail investors who back the wrong companies for the wrong reasons are helping to kill the London market

top_signals.title: Crypto kidnappers who robbed a Minnesota family of $8M plead guilty
top_signals.summary: Two Texas brothers admitted to holding a Minnesota family at gunpoint and forcing the transfer of $8 million in cryptocurrency.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Japan to raise visa fees for foreign nationals in first revision in 48 years
top_signals.summary: The fee will be increased from the current ¥3,000 to ¥15,000 for a single-entry visa and from ¥6,000 to ¥30,000 for a multiple-entry visa starting July 1.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: BMT, Austal Sign Engineering Alliance to Support Shipbuilding Projects
top_signals.summary: Maritime engineering consultancy BMT and Australian shipbuilder Austal have signed a strategic agreement aimed at expanding ship design and engineering capacity while supporting the delivery of commercial and defense shipbuilding programs.The…
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: NYK Group’s ICO Launches Belgium’s First Shore Power Facility for RoRo Ships
top_signals.summary: International Car Operators (ICO), a member of Japan’s NYK Group, has started commercial operations at Belgium’s first shore power facility for roll-on/roll-off (RoRo) vessels at the port of Zeebrugge.The facility entered service after a testing phase earlier this month…
